name: Apprisen - Warren
description_short: Apprisen is a national nonprofit credit counseling agency offering debt management plans, housing counseling, and financial education since 1955, with low fees and HUD/NFCC accreditation.
description_long:
Apprisen has been operating as a nonprofit credit counseling organization since 1955, providing financial guidance to consumers nationwide. The organization is headquartered in Gahanna, Ohio, and has established itself as an accredited member of both the National Foundation for Credit Counseling (NFCC) and the Council on Accreditation (COA). It holds HUD licensing for housing counseling and maintains strong nonprofit ratings, including a 97% score on Charity Navigator and 4.89 out of 5 stars on the Better Business Bureau.

The company offers a comprehensive suite of financial counseling services designed to address various debt and credit challenges. Services include debt management plans (DMPs) with enrollment fees up to $45 and monthly fees up to $45, housing counseling, bankruptcy counseling, student loan guidance, and general credit counseling. All services begin with free consultations, and fees are offered on a sliding scale basis with reductions or waivers available for those experiencing financial hardship.

Apprisen distinguishes itself through its affordability relative to competitors, with DMP fees lower than industry average, and its diversity of service offerings to handle different types of debt beyond just consumer credit. The organization has received recognition from Investopedia as the "Best Overall Credit Counseling Agency" for April 2026, citing low fees, variety of services, and excellent customer reviews as key factors. Its long operational history since 1955 provides substantial experience with various financial situations.

The primary limitation is that certain counseling services are offered only remotely, and on-demand coaching services carry additional fees. Weekend customer support hours are not available, which may inconvenience some consumers. The company's website content was incomplete during research, limiting full visibility into all service details and current offerings.
pros: Low DMP fees (up to $45 enrollment and $45 monthly) compared to industry average
Fees can be reduced or waived for those experiencing financial hardship
Free initial consultation for all services
Excellent customer ratings: 4.89/5 on BBB with ~80 reviews and 97% on Charity Navigator
NFCC and COA accreditation plus HUD licensing for housing counseling
Nationwide availability with experience serving diverse financial situations since 1955
Multiple service types including debt management, housing, bankruptcy, and student loan counseling
cons: Weekend customer support hours are not available
Certain types of counseling services only offered remotely
Additional fees charged for on-demand coaching services
Website navigation appears limited or incomplete based on page-not-found errors during research
best_for: Consumers with overwhelming unsecured debt seeking affordable debt management plans
Homeowners needing housing counseling or at risk of foreclosure
Individuals considering bankruptcy who want professional guidance before filing
Low-income consumers who qualify for fee reductions or waivers
diagnosis: Apprisen is best suited for consumers with moderate to high unsecured debt who want professional guidance from an accredited nonprofit with low fees and financial hardship accommodations. The main caveat is that certain services are remote-only and weekend support is unavailable, which may limit accessibility for some consumers.
services: Free debt and credit counseling consultations
Debt Management Plans (DMPs) with negotiated lower interest rates
Sliding scale fee structures for various counseling services
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 1601 Motor Inn Dr, Ste 225, Girard, OH 44420
meta_description: Apprisen offers debt management plans and financial education with HUD/NFCC accreditation.
